dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for possible lacerations and reported suicide attempt at address in [CITY].
Hi, my name is Jordan, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
Our officer found her outside with a cut on her neck. She's saying, Oh God, I can't tell if this is happening again, and is very upset.
Thank you. Can you tell me where it hurts the most?
My throat from the side. I cut myself because I couldn't take it anymore, and I wanted to end it.
What is your name, and what city are we in right now?
I am Mary Smith. We're in [CITY].
What is today's date? What happened today?
It's June 5th. I tried to kill myself after my boyfriend hurt me again.
What is 2 plus 2? Are you alert and oriented?
Four. I think I'm in [CITY].
Good. Do you know who I am and how you got to this spot?
Yes, you're the EMT. I'm right outside my home on William Street.
Can you talk or feel anything blocking your airway?
Airway is open and self-maintained. No sounds of obstruction.
No signs of compromise. Airway looks good for now.
Let's check your breathing. Can you take a few deep breaths for us?
Breathing is non-labored. Rate is 24 breaths per minute. Looks unlabored.
Oxygen saturation reading is at 100%. Taking another round.
Any issues with how you breathe? Any chest tightness or numbness?
No, just that I took a lot of pills. I think it was something called Ativan and Klonopin.
Let's take a quick look at your pulse and skin condition. Okay?
Pulse is strong and regular, radial. Skin is warm and dry.
Blood pressure is next. Just a quick cuff around your arm?
Initial heart rate is 101 beats per minute.
Breathing rate remains at 24 breaths per minute.
Blood pressure reading is 144 over 119 mmHg-elevated.
Oxygen saturation remains at 100%, normal.
Blood glucose cannot be measured due to testing constraints.
I just want to confirm your mental alertness again. We've got a Glasgow Coma Scale result of 15 (eye opening, verbal response, motor response all normal), which means you're alert and oriented.
Can you tell me when this injury happened?
It was about 4 PM. He noticed the cut and dragged me into the house. He called 911 then left.
Thank you. Did you take something you weren't supposed to today?
Yes, I took Ativan and Klonopin, probably hundreds of pills. They don't help me anymore.
About what you last had to eat or drink?
I'm not sure exactly, but it was tea maybe. I was confused most of the day.
Have you seen healthcare for similar things recently?
Yes, I was at Tucker's a week ago after he hurt me again with the cat. I was extremely upset but didn't try anything like this.
Do you have any medication or food allergies?
I have an allergy to Bactrim and Clonidine. I get a rash with Bactrim.
Are you allergic to anything else, such as latex?
Yes, I have a latex allergy.
What medications do you take regularly besides Ativan and Klonopin?
Just those. They don't do the job, and I feel numb.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how bad is the pain from the cut?
It's a 4, throbbing and burning, just on my throat's right side.
Does it go further or stay put?
No, it stays exactly where I cut.
I need to inspect any other signs of injury or scarring on your skin.
Laceration is approximately 5 to 7 cm long. No active bleeding observed at site. No other rashes, bruises, or scars seen.
Can I look at your eyes now? I'll use a light to check your pupils.
No standard tool is available, but verbal responses and memory are intact.
I'm going to start cleaning the wound with gauze and normal saline.
Wound is cleaned and dressed with 4x4 gauze and clear tape.
Respiratory rate remains at 24, average depth.
Heart rate decreased to 78, still strong.
Blood pressure is now 117 over 82.
Exit to protocol 4-1 - General Trauma Management is the appropriate plan.
Watching closely as SpO₂ drops to 94. No spikes.
Blood glucose remains not obtainable.
ECG shows a change now to 4 lead monitoring.
Heart rate is at 72 with sinus rhythm.
Care will transfer to the [COMPANY_NAME] officer shortly for hospital transport.
ECO officer's signature is obtained. I will work with EMS to get the patient to the hospital.
Later BP reading is 115 over 77.
You mentioned ambulating from the steps outside. How did that feel?
Okay. I had some pain but managed to walk with support. I can walk now with help.
Great. So, moving your own body and adjusting is possible with assistance.
Rechecking breathing now, still 24, no signs of distress or secondary complications.
Circulation remains stable with no signs of shock or bleeding.
Connecting up to the EKG now-it displays a sinus rhythm currently.
Moving carefully to the stretcher so we can monitor and prepare transport.
Finding a stable and safe position for you on the bench-let's secure the straps.
Rechecking wound dressing now. No signs of re-bleeding or adjustment needed.
Wound dressing still held by gauze and tape. No new concerns.
Checking again for any other signs like rashes or improvement.
Skin overall is smooth, warm, and even with the one laceration site.
Just to confirm, you're allergic to Bactrim (Sulfamethoxazole Trimethoprim). Is that for sure?
Yes. Every time I take it, I break out. It's not worth the risk.
SpO₂ is 94. Stable and no new changes.
Glucose remains out of reach for now.
Any changes in laceration pain since last check in?
Still at 4. Not better or worse.
I'll be with you for the rest of the ride. Let's start moving to the transport vehicle.
Holding steady as we secure the gurney and start toward the transport vehicle.
You will transport with the [COMPANY_NAME]. Any questions about that?
No, he is authorized. I'm safe with him too. This time they will help.
Destiny is set as Community Hospital for expert care and health restoration.
Safety belts engaged. No signs of distress during this phase so far.
Heart rate remains at 72 with a stable, monitored rhythm.
Let's quickly reaffirm the last two readings for SpO₂ and BP.
No sudden pain shifts reported. No new symptoms observed.
Blood pressure is at 115 over 77. Pulse and oxygen consistent.
We're progressing to transport safely, with all protocols active and attended.
Let me know if any discomfort increases or if something feels unstable.
It's fine. I feel watched but okay. Thank you for helping.
Finalize and transport per protocol 4-1, everything stable so far.
Recheck complete. Vital signs stable. No deflections or changes.
All secured. EMS and [COMPANY_NAME] will provide transport as needed.
Ready for next steps. Let's begin the transport to the facility now.
Transport initiated. Maintain all vitals and communications for review.
Patient transferred to transport, all duties clear and clean.
Final brief before the report: no new signs, no new data, no new events.
No more interventions needed on scene, patient now en route.
En route status confirmed. No emergency action required further.
